Package neqsim.process.diagnostics

Class TripEvent

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.diagnostics.TripEvent
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class TripEvent extends Object implements Serializable
Represents a detected trip event on a piece of process equipment.

A trip event captures the equipment name, the parameter that triggered it, the threshold that was exceeded, and the actual value at the time of the trip. It also records a timestamp and a severity classification.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Constructor Details

    • TripEvent

      public TripEvent(String equipmentName, String parameterName, double threshold, double actualValue, boolean highTrip, double simulationTimeSeconds, TripEvent.Severity severity)
      Creates a trip event.
      Parameters:
      equipmentName - name of the equipment that tripped
      parameterName - name of the parameter that triggered the trip
      threshold - the threshold value that was exceeded
      actualValue - the actual measured value at the time of the trip
      highTrip - true if this was a high-limit trip, false for low-limit
      simulationTimeSeconds - time in the simulation when the trip occurred
      severity - severity level of the trip

      Gets the actual value at the time of the trip.

    • isHighTrip

      public boolean isHighTrip()
      Checks if this was a high-limit trip.
      Returns:
      true if high-limit trip, false if low-limit trip

    • getTimestampMillis

      public long getTimestampMillis()
      Gets the wall-clock timestamp when the trip was detected.
      Returns:
      timestamp in milliseconds since epoch

    • getSimulationTimeSeconds

      public double getSimulationTimeSeconds()
      Gets the simulation time when the trip occurred.

      Gets the severity level.

      Gets the deviation from the threshold.
